response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which makes his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David gathered three times as many as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together they have 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. That totals 76 blueberries in all.

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch of muffins required 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make divide the total number of blueberries by 9. This gives them 8 full batches with 4 blueberries left over. They sold each batch for $5. So they earned 8 times 5 which is $40 from the muffins.

***

The leftover blueberries were not enough to make another batch. They had 4 blueberries remaining which could not be used for baking. The money they made from the muffins was $40. This amount came from the sale of the batches they made with the collected blueberries. The total number of blueberries they had was 76 and they used 72 of them for the muffins.

***

The process of collecting and selling the blueberries involved several steps. First they gathered the blueberries then they made the muffins. After that they sold the batches. The total money they made was $40. This was the result of their efforts and the sale of the muffins. Their youth was marked by these activities and the outcome of their work.
